rationale.claude: No hook — the letter opens with the candidate's background instead of a connection to Arize. Paragraph 1 ends 'I also handled help desk ticket requests, onboarding documentation, and SOPs and runbooks.' Paragraph 2 immediately starts 'At BEAT THE BOMB, I handled help desk ticket requests, wrote onboarding documentation, and built SOPs and runbooks.' Same three items, second time wrapped in 'At BEAT THE BOMB' — the writer wrote the same sentence twice and the editor didn't catch it. The remaining paragraphs are competent but generic; nothing in the letter tells the reader why Arize specifically vs any of the candidate's other targets. Closing 'I would welcome a conversation' is the boilerplate ending.

key_strengths: Mentions specific Arize-adjacent tools (Atlassian, GitHub user access, Kandji MDM, Bitdefender, VPN troubleshooting) — these show some research into the role's tech stack
Doesn't fabricate claims — sticks to truth-table content
key_weaknesses: Paragraph 1 ('I also handled help desk ticket requests, onboarding documentation, and SOPs and runbooks') and paragraph 2 ('At BEAT THE BOMB, I handled help desk ticket requests, wrote onboarding documentation, and built SOPs and runbooks') restate the same three items back-to-back
No company-specific hook — could be sent to any IT support role with one find/replace
Opening paragraph leads with candidate background ('My background is in...') instead of a connection to Arize's work
Closing is generic boilerplate ('I would welcome a conversation', 'Thank you for your time')
The 'documentation that keeps recurring issues from coming back' phrase reads as a generic AI-summary, not a candidate-specific insight
specific_fixes: Drop paragraph 2's opening — collapse paragraphs 1 and 2 into one paragraph that says it ONCE
Add a real opening hook tied to Arize's product (ML observability platform — what about it draws the candidate?)
Replace 'I would welcome a conversation' with something more specific to next steps the role expects
Cut 'documentation that keeps recurring issues from coming back' — replace with a concrete example of what was documented and what that prevented
tier_definition_match: Matches weak (~6): the building blocks are there but the editorial discipline isn't. Same content stated twice in adjacent paragraphs is the kind of error a recruiter notices immediately, and the absence of a company-specific hook signals 'this letter is a template.' Not bad enough for ~3 (it's coherent and truthful) but well below mid (it has a clear surface defect).
